layman_headline: The crypto market is bleeding — no good place to jump in right now.
layman_explanation: The entire crypto market is in a fearful downturn, with Bitcoin dropping below $62,000 and most altcoins down 7-15% over the past week. The Fear & Greed Index sits at 28 ('Fear'), and Bitcoin dominance is high at 56%, meaning money is flowing out of smaller coins and into Bitcoin as a relative safe haven. None of the swing trade candidates present a compelling risk/reward setup — they're either in confirmed downtrends, overbought after sharp spikes, or lack sufficient chart data to trade with confidence.
layman_action: Stay in cash and be patient. The market is still searching for a bottom, and catching falling knives is how traders lose money. Wait for fear to peak and clear reversal signals to form before committing capital.
expert_reasoning: Every candidate fails at least one critical filter. GRASS has the best mean-reversion argument (-32.8% in 7 days) but the bearish engulfing candle, price below all MAs, and 0.48x risk/reward ratio make it a trap. LIT's RSI at 72.35 is firmly overbought after a massive 55.8% 5-day run — entering here would be chasing. MON's 0.46x risk/reward is unacceptable for swing trading. VELVET's +26% daily move is already extended. BTC dominance at 56% is hostile to altcoin longs. The Fear & Greed improving trend (24.6 → 28) is a small positive, but insufficient without confirming technical setups. The prudent move is to preserve capital and wait for either BTC to establish clear support or for a high-quality oversold bounce setup with proper risk/reward above 2x.
risk_factors: BTC could break below $60K on geopolitical escalation (Hormuz Strait tensions), dragging all alts further down
High BTC dominance (56%) means altcoins face continued outflows even if BTC stabilizes
Fear & Greed at 28 is improving but hasn't reached extreme fear levels (~15) that historically mark reliable bottoms
watch_list: BTC price action around $60,000 — a break below could trigger cascading liquidations and create better entry opportunities
Fear & Greed Index movement — a spike to 'Extreme Fear' (below 20) combined with volume capitulation would signal a better time to buy
GRASS price at $0.30 support — if it holds and forms a reversal pattern with volume, it could become an actionable mean-reversion trade
market_context: Crypto markets are in a broad risk-off selloff with BTC down 2.6% and most alts down 7-15% weekly. Geopolitical risks (Hormuz Strait) are pressuring risk assets, though CoinDesk notes Bitcoin panic-selling may be nearing exhaustion as seller profit margins compress.
context_banner: Market-wide fear and weak technicals across all candidates make capital preservation the highest-conviction play right now.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 28 ('Fear') with an improving trend from 24.6 weekly avg — not yet at extreme fear levels that mark reliable bottoms.
Bitcoin panic-selling may be ending as sellers' profit margins disappear — early sign of capitulation exhaustion but unconfirmed.
Bitcoin threatens $62K amid geopolitical risk as Trump comments on Hormuz Strait, adding macro uncertainty to an already weak market.
LIT RSI at 72 (overbought, avoid), MON RSI at 41 (approaching oversold but risk/reward too poor at 0.46x), all others neutral — no clean oversold bounce setups.
KITE bearish marubozu (-1.4 bias), GRASS bearish engulfing (-0.9 bias), VELVET bearish marubozu (-0.3 bias) — dominant bearish candlestick patterns across candidates.
